entry.title: Re: Planned Orders list of the last MRP run
entry.content:
Hello @Suresh_Eswaran 

Thanks for your question. Certainly! In SAP S/4HANA Cloud, managing and filtering planned orders after an MRP run can be crucial for efficient planning and execution. Here are a few approaches to meet your requirement:

1. Using the "Manage Planned Orders" App with Filters: The "Manage Planned Orders" app in SAP S/4HANA Cloud allows you to filter and sort planned orders based on various criteria. Although it shows all planned orders, you can use the following steps to filter out the newly created planned orders:Open the "Manage Planned Orders" App - Navigate to the app from your Fiori Launchpad.Use the filter options to set the creation date to today’s date. This will help you filter out the planned orders created by the last MRP run.
- You can also filter by priority if you have a custom field or a way to identify priority planned orders. Then Save this filter as a variant for quick access in the future.

2.Using Custom CDS Views: If the standard apps do not meet your requirements, you can create a custom CDS (Core Data Services) view to fetch the planned orders created by the last MRP run. Use the Custom CDS Views app to create a new view (Include the necessary fields such as planned order number, creation date, and priority) - Add a filter condition to only include planned orders created on the current date. Then  Expose this CDS view as an OData service or use it in a custom analytical query.

3. Using Analytical Queries:  You can also create an analytical query to fetch the planned orders created by the last MRP run.Use the "Custom Analytical Queries" app to create a new query. Select the data source related to planned orders(Add a filter for the creation date to today’s date) Optionally, add filters for priority if needed. Save the query and execute it to get the list of planned orders created by the last MRP run.

By using these three approaches, you can effectively filter and manage planned orders created by the last MRP run, ensuring that planners can focus on priority orders.

Hope it can help.

Best Regards

Dave

entry.title: Re: How to change in house production duration
entry.content:
Hello @Ariel09 ,

Thanks for your question. The issue you're encountering with the in-house production duration and processing time being set to 999 days is likely due to a configuration or data inconsistency. Here are some steps you can take to troubleshoot and resolve this issue:

1. Check Routing and Work Center Data:
- Ensure that the routing and work center data are correctly maintained. The processing time in the routing should be correctly defined for each operation.
- Verify that the work center capacity and available time are correctly set up.

2.Review Scheduling Parameters:

- Check the scheduling parameters in the routing. Ensure that the scheduling type and times are correctly defined.
- Verify the scheduling margin key and any other relevant scheduling parameters.

3.Material Master Data:

- Double-check the material master data to ensure that the in-house production time is set to 0 as you mentioned.
- Ensure that the procurement type and special procurement type are correctly set.

4.Check Production Version:
- If you are using production versions, ensure that the correct production version is selected and that it has the correct routing and BOM.

5. Capacity Planning:
- Verify that the capacity planning settings are correctly configured. Ensure that the work center has sufficient capacity to handle the production load.

6. **Customizing Settings:**
- Check the customizing settings for production scheduling  (Implementation Activities). Ensure that the settings for lead time scheduling and capacity planning are correctly configured.

7. Check for User Exits or Enhancements:
- If there are any user exits or enhancements implemented in your system, ensure that they are not affecting the scheduling calculations.

By systematically checking these areas, you should be able to identify and resolve the issue with the in-house production duration and processing time being set to 999 days.

Best Regards

Dave

entry.title: 11- Winner -game-cusTomer-care-helpline–number +91)09547571974_Call me. Hh

entry.title: Re: EHSM Incident Management Field Control Not Working
entry.content:
Dear Jeff,

the configuration/customizing you did in SPRO looks fine from my point of view. If I understand it correctly, you want to hide a number of fields on the Asset tab for Vehicles if the event recorded is a near miss or safety observation.

I recreated the same configuration on our system and it works without any issues.

There is one little thing you might want to try:

  1. Open the transaction SHMM,
  2. Select the shared memory area CL_EHFND_FIELD_CONTROL_SHM,
  3. Invalidate or delete this shared memory area,
  4. Refresh (F5) the near miss or safety observation record you are looking at.

If this does not fix the issue, I would ask you to check if there are any overlapping field control profiles available.

With kind regards,

Michael
